permissionDefinition.code: Unique key used by route and page guards. Prefer stable snake_case or dot notation.
permissionDefinition.name: Display name shown to administrators when managing permissions.
permissionDefinition.description: Optional internal description for this permission.
permissionDefinition.isActive: Inactive permissions are ignored when user access is evaluated.
permissionDefinition.save: Changes affect role and group authorization flows.

permissionGroup.name: Group name used for identification when assigning to users.
permissionGroup.isSystemAdmin: Members of this group can access all pages and actions without individual permission checks.
permissionGroup.isActive: Inactive groups should not be assigned for new access decisions.
permissionGroup.permissions: Selected permissions define what members of this group can access.
permissionGroup.save: Updates effective access for every user assigned to this group.

userAssignment.user: Select the user whose access scope you want to manage.
userAssignment.groups: The user receives the combined permissions from all selected groups.
userAssignment.save: Assignments take effect immediately for the next authorization checks. Users may need to refresh their session.
userAssignment.systemAdminNote: If the user belongs to a system administrator group, detailed permissions are skipped.

visibilityPolicy.code: Use a unique code so the policy can be recognized in assignment and audit flows.
visibilityPolicy.name: Display name that administrators will see while managing the policy.
visibilityPolicy.includeSelf: When enabled, the user's own records remain visible within this scope.
visibilityPolicy.isActive: Inactive policies stay out of new assignments and access decisions.

userVisibilityAssignment.user: Select the user whose entity visibility you want to manage.
userVisibilityAssignment.policy: Choose one active visibility policy per entity or leave it empty.

permissionDefinitions.description: Manage permission codes used across the system

form.addDescription: Create a new visibility policy definition
form.editDescription: Update the selected visibility policy settings

userVisibilityAssignments.description: Manage entity visibility policy assignments per user
userVisibilityAssignments.selectUserHint: Select a user to manage visibility assignments
userVisibilityAssignments.cardDescription: Choose the visibility policy that will be applied to {{entity}} records for this user.

visibilitySimulator.title: Visibility simulator
visibilitySimulator.description: Test which users' records and active approval override records are visible to the selected user for the selected entity type.
visibilitySimulator.helpUser: Select the user whose visibility you want to simulate.
visibilitySimulator.helpEntity: Select the module for which you want to simulate record visibility.
visibilitySimulator.helpRecordId: Enter a specific record ID to simulate whether view, update, delete, and approve actions are allowed.
visibilitySimulator.policyActive: An active visibility policy is applied
visibilitySimulator.awaitingSelection: Select a user and entity first
visibilitySimulator.noOverrides: No active approval override records found
visibilitySimulator.noAudit: No active approval override audit entries found
visibilitySimulator.noRecordSimulation: When you enter a record ID, the view / update / delete / approve result will appear here.

auditLogs.title: Audit logs
auditLogs.description: Track who performed critical actions, under which trace, and how they ended.

detail.title: Audit log detail
